title: Kartikeya Chalisa
intro: Shri Kartikeya Chalisa - Hymn to Lord Kartikeya
description:
॥ Kartikeya Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Jai Kartikeya deva prabhu, Shadanana gunadhama.
Shiva suvana shakti dhara, karahu sakala shubha kama.
Gauri lala mahavira, devasenapati nama.
Mayura vahana shobhate, kijai mora pranama.

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jai jai Kartikeya gunasagara.
Shiva Parvati suta sukhadagara.
Shadamukha shobhita chhabi ati pyari.
Dwadasha bhuja bala shakti apari.
Mayura vahana shobhata bhari.
Swarna kavacha tana jyoti ujari.
Shakti amogha trishula viraje.
Devasenapati veshabhusha saje.
Tarakasura sana yuddha machayo.
Bala parakrama saba jaga dikhlayo.
Krittikaon ne pala pose.
Kartikeya nama taba dhare.
Skanda Kumara Subrahmanya kahaye.
Murugan nama sakala jaga gaye.
Agni teja se prakata bhaye tum.
Deva danuja saba namata hai tum.
Ganga ji ne kiya sahara.
Sharavana vana mein rupa nikhara.
Chaha mukha dhari adbhuta rupa.
Anupama teja divya swarupa.
Devataon ke kashta nivare.
Tarakasura ko vadha sanhare.
Krauncha parvata tuma bhedana kina.
Dushta daitya ka bala hara lina.
Devasena patni tumhari.
Valli sanga shobha atibhari.
Kailasa parvata nivasa tumhara.
Palani kshetra sushobhita dwara.
Bhaktana ko sada sukha dete.
Sankata harata kripa barasete.
Shatru vinashaka vira mahavira.
Sharana pade ko deta abhaya pira.
Jnana vijnana viveka pradata.
Sakala vidya ke tuma ho gyata.
Om Sharavanabhavaya namo namo.
Swaminatha tumhein shata shata pranamo.
Graha dosha nivarana karta.
Mangala dayaka bhaya ke harta.
Kuja dosha saba dura bhagave.
Kartikeya jo dhyana lagave.
Senani prabhu devana ke nayaka.
Sura dala pati saba jana hitakayaka.
Parakrami balavana apara.
Karata sada dushtana samhara.
Mata Gauri ke lala dulare.
Pita Shambhu ke nayana ke tare.
Ganesha bhrata prema nikhare.
Dono bhai jagata pujare.
Shadbhuja shaktidhara vela viraje.
Manimaya mukuta lalata para saje.
Kundala karna virajata sohe.
Darshana karata mana sabake mohe.
Nila vastra shobha ati bhari.
Pushpamala gala shobhata pyari.
Sahasa shaurya parakrama data.
Rana mein ajaya amita sukhatrata.
Santana sukha ke vara pradata.
Nissantana ke dukha ke harta.
Vivaha yogya vara data swami.
Kamana puraka antaryami.
Tamila desha mein Murugan kahaye.
Karodon bhaktana sharana mein aye.
Tiruchendura mein dhwaja faharave.
Palani mein darshana de sukha pave.
Swamimalai shikhara para viraje.
Bhaktana ke mana mohana saje.
Shashthi tithi ko vrata jo karta.
Sakala manoratha purana bharta.
Somavara ko pujana kariye.
Kartikeya ka dhyana sada dharaye.
Shakti swarupa sanatana deva.
Karata sakala jaga tumhari seva.
Papa tapa saba dura bhagave.
Mana ki ichha purna karave.
Jo koi chalisa gave.
Kartikeya ko dhyana lagave.
Sakala bighana usake mita jahi.
Manavanchhita phala avashya hi pahi.

॥ Doha ॥
Kartikeya prabhu sharana mein, jo nara kare pranama.
Sakala siddhi so pavahi, purana hoya saba kama.
Shadanana ki chalisa, padhe bhakti hita laya.
Shatru vinasha sukha sampada, mangala sada sahaya.
meaning:
॥ Kartikeya Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Jai Kartikeya deva prabhu, Shadanana gunadhama.
[Victory to Lord Kartikeya, the six-faced one, the abode of virtues.]
Shiva suvana shakti dhara, karahu sakala shubha kama.
[Son of Shiva, wielder of power, fulfill all my auspicious deeds.]
Gauri lala mahavira, devasenapati nama.
[Beloved son of Gauri, the great warrior, commander of the divine army.]
Mayura vahana shobhate, kijai mora pranama.
[You look magnificent on your peacock mount; I offer my salutations to you.]

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jai jai Kartikeya gunasagara.
[Victory to Kartikeya, the ocean of virtues.]
Shiva Parvati suta sukhadagara.
[Son of Shiva and Parvati, the giver of happiness.]
Shadamukha shobhita chhabi ati pyari.
[Your six faces look very beautiful.]
Dwadasha bhuja bala shakti apari.
[Twelve arms with infinite strength and power.]
Mayura vahana shobhata bhari.
[You look magnificent mounted on your peacock.]
Swarna kavacha tana jyoti ujari.
[Golden armor on your body shines brightly.]
Shakti amogha trishula viraje.
[You possess the invincible Shakti weapon and the trident.]
Devasenapati veshabhusha saje.
[Dressed in the attire of the commander of the divine army.]
Tarakasura sana yuddha machayo.
[You waged war against Tarakasura.]
Bala parakrama saba jaga dikhlayo.
[You showed your strength and valor to the whole world.]
Krittikaon ne pala pose.
[Raised and nurtured by the Krittikas.]
Kartikeya nama taba dhare.
[That is how you came to be named Kartikeya.]
Skanda Kumara Subrahmanya kahaye.
[You are called Skanda, Kumara, and Subrahmanya.]
Murugan nama sakala jaga gaye.
[The whole world sings your name as Murugan.]
Agni teja se prakata bhaye tum.
[You appeared from the brilliance of fire.]
Deva danuja saba namata hai tum.
[Gods and demons all bow down to you.]
Ganga ji ne kiya sahara.
[Mother Ganga supported you.]
Sharavana vana mein rupa nikhara.
[You blossomed in the Sharavana forest.]
Chaha mukha dhari adbhuta rupa.
[Your six-faced form is wondrous.]
Anupama teja divya swarupa.
[Your brilliance is incomparable and divine.]
Devataon ke kashta nivare.
[You removed the sufferings of the gods.]
Tarakasura ko vadha sanhare.
[You destroyed Tarakasura.]
Krauncha parvata tuma bhedana kina.
[You pierced the Krauncha mountain.]
Dushta daitya ka bala hara lina.
[You took away the strength of the wicked demons.]
Devasena patni tumhari.
[Devasena is your wife.]
Valli sanga shobha atibhari.
[You look magnificent with Valli.]
Kailasa parvata nivasa tumhara.
[Your abode is Mount Kailash.]
Palani kshetra sushobhita dwara.
[The Palani region is your glorified abode.]
Bhaktana ko sada sukha dete.
[You always give happiness to your devotees.]
Sankata harata kripa barasete.
[You remove crises and shower your grace.]
Shatru vinashaka vira mahavira.
[Destroyer of enemies, the great warrior.]
Sharana pade ko deta abhaya pira.
[You give fearlessness to those who seek refuge.]
Jnana vijnana viveka pradata.
[Giver of knowledge, science, and wisdom.]
Sakala vidya ke tuma ho gyata.
[You are the knower of all knowledge.]
Om Sharavanabhavaya namo namo.
[Salutations to the one born in the Sharavana forest.]
Swaminatha tumhein shata shata pranamo.
[I bow to you hundreds of times, O Swaminatha.]
Graha dosha nivarana karta.
[Remover of planetary afflictions.]
Mangala dayaka bhaya ke harta.
[Giver of auspiciousness and destroyer of fear.]
Kuja dosha saba dura bhagave.
[You remove all Kuja (Mars) defects.]
Kartikeya jo dhyana lagave.
[For those who meditate on Kartikeya.]
Senani prabhu devana ke nayaka.
[Commander, Lord, and leader of the gods.]
Sura dala pati saba jana hitakayaka.
[Leader of the divine army, well-wisher of all.]
Parakrami balavana apara.
[Valorous and infinitely strong.]
Karata sada dushtana samhara.
[Always destroying the wicked.]
Mata Gauri ke lala dulare.
[Beloved son of Mother Gauri.]
Pita Shambhu ke nayana ke tare.
[Apple of Father Shambhu's eye.]
Ganesha bhrata prema nikhare.
[Brother of Ganesha, your love is pure.]
Dono bhai jagata pujare.
[Both brothers are worshipped by the world.]
Shadbhuja shaktidhara vela viraje.
[Six-armed and wielder of the Vel weapon.]
Manimaya mukuta lalata para saje.
[Adorned with a gem-studded crown on your forehead.]
Kundala karna virajata sohe.
[Earrings look beautiful on your ears.]
Darshana karata mana sabake mohe.
[Your sight captivates the minds of all.]
Nila vastra shobha ati bhari.
[Adorned in blue garments.]
Pushpamala gala shobhata pyari.
[A beautiful flower garland looks lovely on your neck.]
Sahasa shaurya parakrama data.
[Giver of courage, valor, and strength.]
Rana mein ajaya amita sukhatrata.
[Invincible in battle, the protector of happiness.]
Santana sukha ke vara pradata.
[Bestower of the boon of children.]
Nissantana ke dukha ke harta.
[Destroyer of the sorrow of the childless.]
Vivaha yogya vara data swami.
[Bestower of suitable spouses, O Lord.]
Kamana puraka antaryami.
[Fulfiller of desires, the knower of hearts.]
Tamila desha mein Murugan kahaye.
[Known as Murugan in the land of Tamil.]
Karodon bhaktana sharana mein aye.
[Millions of devotees have come to your refuge.]
Tiruchendura mein dhwaja faharave.
[Your flag flies high in Tiruchendur.]
Palani mein darshana de sukha pave.
[You give joy by showing yourself in Palani.]
Swamimalai shikhara para viraje.
[You reside on the peak of Swamimalai.]
Bhaktana ke mana mohana saje.
[You look charming in the hearts of devotees.]
Shashthi tithi ko vrata jo karta.
[Whoever observes the fast on the Shashthi day.]
Sakala manoratha purana bharta.
[Their all wishes are fulfilled.]
Somavara ko pujana kariye.
[Worship on Mondays.]
Kartikeya ka dhyana sada dharaye.
[Always meditate on Kartikeya.]
Shakti swarupa sanatana deva.
[Eternal God, the embodiment of power.]
Karata sakala jaga tumhari seva.
[The whole world serves you.]
Papa tapa saba dura bhagave.
[You remove all sins and sorrows.]
Mana ki ichha purna karave.
[You fulfill the desires of the mind.]
Jo koi chalisa gave.
[Whoever sings this Chalisa.]
Kartikeya ko dhyana lagave.
[And meditates on Kartikeya.]
Sakala bighana usake mita jahi.
[All their obstacles will be removed.]
Manavanchhita phala avashya hi pahi.
[They will certainly get their desired fruits.]

॥ Doha ॥
Kartikeya prabhu sharana mein, jo nara kare pranama.
[The person who bows to Lord Kartikeya in refuge.]
Sakala siddhi so pavahi, purana hoya saba kama.
[They attain all perfections, and all their works are completed.]
Shadanana ki chalisa, padhe bhakti hita laya.
[He who reads the Chalisa of the six-faced one with devotion.]
Shatru vinasha sukha sampada, mangala sada sahaya.
[Destruction of enemies, happiness, and prosperity always assist them.]

faq.q1: What is the main significance of the Kartikeya Chalisa?
faq.a1: Lord Kartikeya (Murugan) is the symbol of courage, victory, discipline, and power. Reciting his Chalisa boosts self-confidence and provides the inner strength to overcome life's obstacles.
faq.a2: Yes, Lord Kartikeya is considered a deity of intellect and concentration. Students who struggle to focus on their studies find the recitation of this Chalisa highly beneficial.
faq.q3: What is the best day to recite it?
faq.a3: Tuesdays and the 'Shashti' Tithi (the sixth day of the lunar month) are considered the best for the worship of Lord Kartikeya.
faq.q4: Does it help in overcoming enemies?
faq.a4: Lord Kartikeya is the commander of the divine army. Remembering him gives the devotee the courage and morale to triumph over enemies and negative forces.
faq.q5: Do I need a formal initiation or complex ritual to read it?
faq.a5: No, Lord Kartikeya listens to the prayers of a pure and sincere heart. Without any formal initiation, you can recite this Chalisa with cleanliness and full dedication.
